vulnerabilities.description: jsonwebtoken unrestricted key type could lead to legacy keys usage

analysis.detail: Application uses jsonwebtoken@8.5.1 for JWT signing/verification. The unrestricted key type vulnerability could allow attackers to use legacy key algorithms. MUST UPGRADE to jsonwebtoken@9.x.

vulnerabilities.description: jsonwebtoken insecure implementation of key retrieval function could lead to Forgeable Public/Private Tokens from RSA to HMAC

analysis.detail: The application uses RS256 with RSA keys. This vulnerability allows HMAC confusion attacks where an attacker could forge tokens using the public key. Critical finding — upgrade required.

vulnerabilities.description: jsonwebtoken vulnerable to signature validation bypass due to insecure default algorithm in jwt.verify()

analysis.detail: The application explicitly specifies the algorithm in jwt.verify() options (RS256), so the insecure default algorithm path is not reachable. However, upgrading is still recommended.

vulnerabilities.description: form-data uses unsafe random function for choosing boundary

analysis.detail: form-data is a transitive dependency of googleapis. The application does not directly use form-data for security-sensitive multipart boundary generation. The weak randomness only affects MIME boundary selection which is not a security boundary in this context.

vulnerabilities.description: express vulnerable to XSS via response.redirect()

analysis.detail: The application API does not use res.redirect() with user-controlled URLs. All redirects are to static paths. Additionally, Nginx handles all user-facing HTTP responses in production, adding defense-in-depth headers.

vulnerabilities.description: Express.js Open Redirect in malformed URLs

analysis.detail: The application does not use res.redirect() with user-supplied input. All routing is handled by explicit route definitions.
ReDoS risk is mitigated by Nginx rate limiting (5 req/s on API, 2 req/s on auth) and Express global rate limiter (100 req/15 min). Route parameters are simple strings, not complex patterns.

vulnerabilities.description: nodemailer ReDoS when trying to send a specially crafted email

analysis.detail: The application uses nodemailer for email sending. A specially crafted email address could trigger ReDoS. Input validation via Joi mitigates but does not eliminate this risk. UPDATE RECOMMENDED.

vulnerabilities.description: Nodemailer: Email to an unintended domain can occur due to Interpretation Conflict

analysis.detail: Interpretation conflict in address parsing could cause emails to be sent to unintended domains. Since the application sends transactional emails, this could be abused for phishing. UPDATE REQUIRED.
braces is a dev/build dependency used by micromatch for glob pattern matching. It is not used at runtime in production and is not exposed to user input.

vulnerabilities.description: Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) in cross-spawn

analysis.detail: cross-spawn is a dev dependency used by cross-env for running npm scripts. It is not used in production runtime and is not exposed to user input.

vulnerabilities.description: flatted vulnerable to unbounded recursion DoS in parse() revive phase

analysis.detail: flatted is a transitive dependency of Winston's logform. It is not used to parse untrusted user input. The application only uses Winston for internal logging output.

vulnerabilities.description: Prototype Pollution via parse() in NodeJS flatted

analysis.detail: flatted.parse() is not called with untrusted input. It's only used internally by Winston for log serialization.

jws is used by jsonwebtoken for JWT operations. Improper HMAC verification could allow token forgery. This is part of the broader jsonwebtoken@8.x vulnerability cluster. Upgrade jsonwebtoken to v9.x to resolve.

axios is used exclusively on the CLIENT side (React SPA) for browser-to-API requests. SSRF is a server-side vulnerability and does not apply to browser-based HTTP clients. The browser's same-origin policy and CORS configuration provide protection.

vulnerabilities.description: Node.js 16 reached End of Life on 2023-09-11 and no longer receives security patches

analysis.detail: Both server and client Dockerfiles use node:16 which has been EOL since September 2023. No security patches are being released for this version. CRITICAL: Upgrade to node:20-alpine (LTS until 2026-04) or node:22-alpine (LTS until 2027-04).
